chapter.title: Fine-Tuning Mastery
chapter.subtitle: Customize foundation models for your domain without a PhD
chapter.description: Master the complete fine-tuning stack — from LoRA math to QLoRA cost optimization, DPO alignment, dataset curation, and production deployment with vLLM and Ollama.

levels.title: When to Fine-Tune
levels.subtitle: RAG vs Fine-Tune vs Hybrid — Decision Framework
levels.key_insight: Fine-tune for FORMAT (JSON output, writing style, classification, structured extraction). RAG for KNOWLEDGE (facts, documents, citations, live data). The decision is about what changes: if the task structure changes → fine-tune; if the facts change → RAG.

content.markdown: <5% of production AI uses full fine-tuning. LoRA/QLoRA is the standard for 95% of cases. Knowing WHEN to fine-tune separates AI engineers from prompt engineers.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Fine-tune for FORMAT (JSON output, writing style, classification, structured extraction). RAG for KNOWLEDGE (facts, documents, citations, live data). The decision is about what changes: if the task structure changes → fine-tune; if the facts change → RAG.

levels.title: SFT Fundamentals
levels.subtitle: Supervised Fine-Tuning — Hyperparameters That Matter

game_config.scenario: Flash Attention 2 delivers 3-10x faster attention computation. Learning rate is the single most critical SFT hyperparameter — too high causes catastrophic forgetting, too low means no adaptation.

levels.key_insight: Start with lr=2e-5, warmup=10% of total steps, 3 epochs. Watch validation loss — when it diverges from training loss, you're overfitting. Flash Attention 2 has zero quality impact; enable it unconditionally for 3-10x speed improvement.

content.markdown: Flash Attention 2 delivers 3-10x faster attention computation. Learning rate is the single most critical SFT hyperparameter — too high causes catastrophic forgetting, too low means no adaptation.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Start with lr=2e-5, warmup=10% of total steps, 3 epochs. Watch validation loss — when it diverges from training loss, you're overfitting. Flash Attention 2 has zero quality impact; enable it unconditionally for 3-10x speed improvement.

levels.title: LoRA Deep Dive
levels.subtitle: The Math Behind Low-Rank Adaptation

game_config.scenario: W = W₀ + BA. B is (d×r), A is (r×k). With r=16, you train 0.1% of parameters while preserving 95%+ of full fine-tuning quality. The rank r controls the expressivity-efficiency tradeoff.

levels.key_insight: r=16, alpha=32 (scaling factor=2x) is the universal LoRA starting point. Target all linear layers (not just attention) for a 5-10% quality boost at 2x parameter cost. Beyond r=64, quality gains are marginal — you're wasting VRAM.

content.markdown: W = W₀ + BA. B is (d×r), A is (r×k). With r=16, you train 0.1% of parameters while preserving 95%+ of full fine-tuning quality. The rank r controls the expressivity-efficiency tradeoff.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: r=16, alpha=32 (scaling factor=2x) is the universal LoRA starting point. Target all linear layers (not just attention) for a 5-10% quality boost at 2x parameter cost. Beyond r=64, quality gains are marginal — you're wasting VRAM.

levels.title: QLoRA Cost Optimizer
levels.subtitle: 4-bit Quantized LoRA — Democratizing Fine-Tuning

game_config.scenario: 7B full fine-tuning = 112GB VRAM → QLoRA = 5GB. 70B full fine-tuning = 560GB VRAM → QLoRA = 46GB. NF4 (NormalFloat4) quantization is information-theoretically optimal for normally distributed neural n

levels.key_insight: 7B QLoRA on RTX 4090 (~$0.40/hr on Vast.ai) = ~$1.20 for 3 hours on 1K samples. QLoRA matches full LoRA quality with negligible degradation from the 4-bit NF4 base model. NF4 beats standard INT4 because it places quantization bins at equal-area intervals matching the normal distribution of LLM weights.

content.markdown: 7B full fine-tuning = 112GB VRAM → QLoRA = 5GB. 70B full fine-tuning = 560GB VRAM → QLoRA = 46GB. NF4 (NormalFloat4) quantization is information-theoretically optimal for normally distributed neural network weights.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: 7B QLoRA on RTX 4090 (~$0.40/hr on Vast.ai) = ~$1.20 for 3 hours on 1K samples. QLoRA matches full LoRA quality with negligible degradation from the 4-bit NF4 base model. NF4 beats standard INT4 because it places quantization bins at equal-area intervals matching the normal distribution of LLM weights.

levels.title: LoRA Variants
levels.subtitle: DoRA, rsLoRA, LoRA+, TIES Merge, DARE Merge
levels.key_insight: Try LoRA+ first — it's just a hyperparameter change (set B's LR 4-16x higher than A's). Then DoRA for low-rank scenarios (r≤8). rsLoRA only matters at r≥64. Use TIES merge when combining task-specific adapters to avoid sign conflicts.

content.markdown: DoRA (Weight-Decomposed LoRA, ICLR 2025) decomposes weight updates into magnitude and direction components, delivering +22% improvement at low ranks with zero inference overhead — it merges back into the base model identically.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Try LoRA+ first — it's just a hyperparameter change (set B's LR 4-16x higher than A's). Then DoRA for low-rank scenarios (r≤8). rsLoRA only matters at r≥64. Use TIES merge when combining task-specific adapters to avoid sign conflicts.

levels.title: DPO Pipeline
levels.subtitle: Direct Preference Optimization — No Reward Model Needed
levels.key_insight: DPO skips Reward Model and PPO entirely. Even 2K high-quality chosen/rejected pairs can produce 5% benchmark improvement. Focus annotation budget on CHOSEN response quality — a mediocre chosen example is worse than no example.

content.markdown: DPO eliminates the separate reward model AND the PPO optimizer entirely by using a mathematically equivalent closed-form loss. Quality of CHOSEN responses is the primary driver — focus annotation budget there, not on chosen/rejected balance.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: DPO skips Reward Model and PPO entirely. Even 2K high-quality chosen/rejected pairs can produce 5% benchmark improvement. Focus annotation budget on CHOSEN response quality — a mediocre chosen example is worse than no example.

levels.title: Alignment Method Selector
levels.subtitle: RLHF, DPO, ORPO, SimPO, GRPO — When to Use Which
levels.key_insight: DPO for simplicity and fast iteration. SimPO for highest benchmark quality. GRPO for reasoning enhancement (math, code, chain-of-thought). ORPO for single-stage efficiency. RLHF is legacy for teams without dedicated RL researchers.

content.markdown: The 2026 production alignment stack: SFT → DPO or SimPO (alignment) → GRPO or RLVR (reasoning). This exact sequence is used by every frontier lab including Llama 3, Qwen 2.5, and DeepSeek.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: DPO for simplicity and fast iteration. SimPO for highest benchmark quality. GRPO for reasoning enhancement (math, code, chain-of-thought). ORPO for single-stage efficiency. RLHF is legacy for teams without dedicated RL researchers.

levels.title: Dataset Curation Pipeline
levels.subtitle: The Secret Weapon — Quality over Quantity
levels.key_insight: Magpie synthetic generation costs 30x less than human curation. Always run benchmark contamination checks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K n-gram overlap) before training — contaminated data inflates eval scores without real improvement. A 10K curated set beats 100K noisy data.

content.markdown: Magpie (ICLR 2025): by prompting Llama 3 with only a user-turn token, the model auto-generates instruction-response pairs. 300K Magpie-filtered examples match the quality of 10M human-curated samples. Data quality beats data quantity every time.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Magpie synthetic generation costs 30x less than human curation. Always run benchmark contamination checks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K n-gram overlap) before training — contaminated data inflates eval scores without real improvement. A 10K curated set beats 100K noisy data.

levels.title: Tools & Deployment
levels.subtitle: Unsloth, Axolotl, vLLM — Production Fine-Tuning Stack
levels.key_insight: Two deployment paths: (1) Merge LoRA → GGUF → Ollama: simple, single-user, edge deployment. (2) Keep LoRA separate → vLLM: multiple adapters sharing one base model in memory (enterprise multi-tenant). For >3 adapters, vLLM's LoRA multiplexing beats merged-model memory overhead.

content.markdown: Unsloth = single-GPU speed king (2-5x faster training via custom CUDA kernels, 70% less VRAM). Axolotl = production pipeline king (YAML-driven, multi-GPU, integrates everything). vLLM for serving multiple LoRA adapters sharing one base model simultaneously.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Two deployment paths: (1) Merge LoRA → GGUF → Ollama: simple, single-user, edge deployment. (2) Keep LoRA separate → vLLM: multiple adapters sharing one base model in memory (enterprise multi-tenant). For >3 adapters, vLLM's LoRA multiplexing beats merged-model memory overhead.

levels.title: Evaluation & Diagnosis
levels.subtitle: Before/After Metrics — Catching Catastrophic Forgetting
levels.key_insight: Fix catastrophic forgetting by mixing 20-30% general instruction data into your fine-tuning dataset. Always track MMLU, safety scores, and perplexity on general text alongside task metrics. The goal is task improvement WITH zero regression on general benchmarks.

content.markdown: Always report DELTA metrics, not just final scores. Evaluate general benchmarks (MMLU, HellaSwag) alongside task-specific ones to catch catastrophic forgetting — the silent killer of fine-tuned models.

content.title: Enterprise Insight
content.content: Fix catastrophic forgetting by mixing 20-30% general instruction data into your fine-tuning dataset. Always track MMLU, safety scores, and perplexity on general text alongside task metrics. The goal is task improvement WITH zero regression on general benchmarks.

levels.title: Interview Gauntlet
levels.subtitle: Boss Level — 20 Expert Fine-Tuning Questions. Score 80% to unlock Chapter 5.

content.markdown: 20 questions covering every dimension of production fine-tuning. These are real questions asked by AI teams at top companies. Score 16/20 (80%) to pass.
